amu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guerrero Amuzgo.
caul: noun. A style of close-fitting circular cap worn by women in the sixteenth century and later, often made of linen.
clam: noun. A bivalve mollusk of many kinds, especially those that are edible; for example soft-shell clams (Mya arenaria), hard clams
clan: noun. A group of people all descended from a common ancestor, in fact or belief, especially when the exact genealogies are not known.
coal: noun. A black or brownish black rock formed from prehistoric plant remains, composed largely of carbon and burned as a fuel.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
cola: noun. The kola plant, genus Cola, famous for its nut, or one of these nuts.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
culm: noun. Waste coal, used as a poor quality fuel.
cum: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Cumeral.
lac: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lacandon.
loam: noun. A type of soil; an earthy mixture of sand, silt and clay, with organic matter to which its fertility is chiefly due.
loan: noun. An act or instance of lending, an act or instance of granting something for temporary use.
mac: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Macedonian.
macon: noun. A type of oar blade with an elliptical shape which is squared off at the end, with a ridgeline running down the centre of the blade
malm: noun. A soft, crumbly, chalky, grayish limestone.
man: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mandingo.
mano: noun. A stone resembling a rolling pin, used to grind maize or other grain on a metate.
maul: noun. A heavy long-handled hammer, used for splitting logs by driving a wedge into them, or in combat.
moa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mwan.
moan: noun. A low, mournful cry of pain, sorrow or pleasure.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
mom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monimbo.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
mum: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maiwala.
mun: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Munda languages.
muon: noun. An unstable elementary particle in the lepton family, having similar properties to the electron but with a mass 207 times greater.
nam: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngan'gityemerri.
oca: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ocaina.
ulna: noun. The bone of the forearm that extends from the elbow to the wrist on the side opposite to the thumb, corresponding to the fibula of
umm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Umon.
